Monday: Margaret opened with: "Jalapeño corn dog bites. We're doing it this week and I need to know right now if anyone thinks this is just nostalgia dressed up as food or if we actually respect the technique here." Marcus and Stephanie convinced her the technique is real - the cornmeal crust seals the jalapeño's moisture, concentrating heat on bite. They decided to anchor the story around this moisture-seal engineering angle, with Marcus leading the copy.

Tuesday: **Jalapeño Corn Dog Bites - Tuesday Summary**

Margaret established the cornmeal-to-flour ratio at 60-40 minimum to prevent seal breakdown during frying. Marcus and Stephanie agreed the copy should respect this technical constraint rather than apologize for it. Margaret: "Marcus gets it. Fine. Cornmeal-to-flour ratio is 60-40 minimum or the seal breaks down in the oil." Team decided to move forward with this approach.

Wednesday: Julian presented three lighting approaches for the jalapeño corn dog bite-through moment. Stephanie pushed for shot two, emphasizing the crisp exterior shattering: "Shot two's the one - if people are stopping their scroll, it's because they see that crisp exterior breaking apart, right?" Margaret agreed, and the team locked in shot two as final.

Thursday: Marcus finalized copy emphasizing how cornmeal's moisture-seal properties affect texture. Stephanie loved the angle but wanted tighter writing to avoid repetition. Margaret and Stephanie agreed to lead with the science of steam absorption during frying, then Marcus revised to cut looping explanations. Decision: Marcus will send the streamlined version focusing on what happens when fried, not why.

Friday: Margaret locked Shot Two and copy, inviting final feedback. The team agreed that trusting the work rather than overthinking it improved results - Marcus noted the science-first approach let writing breathe, while Julian credited restraint in cinematography. Stephanie highlighted that listening instead of defending positions made the difference.

Saturday: Devon completed verification and pushed the Jalapeño Corn Dog Bites to live. Margaret and Devon agreed that restraint in design and execution keeps things clean and functional. No disagreements or unresolved questions emerged.
